summary: Overview Title V of the Social Security Act (SSA; P.L. 74-121, as amended; 42 U.S.C. §§701-709) permanently authorizes the Maternal and Child Health (MCH) Services Block Grant, which aims to improve the health of pregnant women, mothers, and children, particularly those with low-income or limited access to health services. title: Expanding Technical and Professional Components to all Medicare Physician Fee Schedule Services
summary: Revising Medicare’s allocation of indirect practice expenses to depend on both physician work and clinical labor for all services would align payments between services with and without separately billable professional and technical components.

title: Technical Documentation for the Thirteenth American School District Panel Survey
summary: This report provides technical information about the spring 2026 survey of the American School District Panel. The authors describe the survey administration and weighting processes they used to produce nationally representative estimates.

title: Shrinking Budgets, Fewer Students
summary: This report presents findings from the spring 2026 American School District Panel survey. Budget shortfalls and declining enrollment have risen to the top of district leaders’ concerns, worsening other challenges that districts are facing.

title: Blind Spot Indicators for Space
summary: In this report, the authors present methodologies for synthesizing space-related research and development efforts by the United States and China to project trends in future capabilities and operations that use space.

title: The U.S.-Iran Ceasefire May Be Over, But It Is Clear What the Regime Wants
summary: The collapse of the memorandum of understanding revealed Iran’s core priority: control of the Strait of Hormuz. But with both sides locked in an escalating battle of wills, the question is whether a lasting deal is still possible.

title: Indonesia’s Warning: When Security Weakens Democracy
summary: Indonesia is turning to its military to address challenges far beyond national defense. But when security institutions substitute for weak civilian governance, gains in state capacity can come at the expense of democratic accountability.

title: A Century Of Ideas: The Crucible Of Citizenship
summary: America was a multiracial, multilanguage, multireligion mixing bowl even before it was the United States. Debates over immigration and citizenship have been part of the American landscape from the start. Citizenship and its most important component— voting rights—have been the most frequent topic of constitutional amendments over the years. Panelists will discuss what it means to be a citizen and what it means to...

title: A Century Of Ideas: Labor And Capital Market Policy: From Ideas To Actions Over One Hundred Years
summary: Nowhere are good economic ideas more important for growth and stability than in labor and capital markets. Enormous technological, political, and demographic shifts in the past one hundred years have changed what is feasible and what works in practice.

title: A Century Of Ideas: Hoover’s One Hundred Years Of War, Revolution, And Peace
summary: Historians Niall Ferguson and Victor Davis Hanson will discuss the seminal events of the last century—the two World Wars, the Great Depression, the rise and fall of Soviet communism, and the advent of modernism and globalization—and how Hoover Institution scholars, informed by the lessons of history, have interpreted these tragedies and challenges.

title: A Century Of Ideas: Tax And Monetary Reform: From Ideas To Actions Over One Hundred Years
summary: Hoover Institution economists have generated many ideas on tax reform and monetary reform over the years, from the Friedman Rule and government spending limits to the Hall-Rabushka Flat Tax and the Taylor Rule. Panelists will discuss the origination of such ideas, how they have been applied in practice in the United States and other countries, and the future of such reform-oriented policy.

title: Maine Sen. King says feds lack credibility, locals need role in ICE shooting investigation

title: Ousted Election Assistance Commission chair on Trump's effort to control voting

title: China’s Transnational Repression Is Being Met With a Deafening Silence
